<h3>USCDI+ Quality Element Support Expectations</h3>

Systems are expected to support this profile as part of implementing USCDI+ Quality V1 in FHIR and meeting the conformance requirements of this guide.

The following elements are specifically relevant to USCDI+ Quality V1 data classes and elements. Systems are expected to support these elements as applicable to their role in US Quality Core data exchange. See the [Must Support](must-support.html) section of this guide for more information. These elements are tagged as (USCDI+ Quality) in the formal profile views.

<!-- START AUTOGENERATED KEY ELEMENTS -->
<b>USCDI+ Quality Elements:</b>
<ul>
<li>status: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) active | on-hold | cancelled | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | draft | unknown</li>
<li>intent: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) proposal | plan | order | original-order | reflex-order | filler-order | instance-order | option</li>
<li>medication[x]: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Medication to be taken</li>
<li>authoredOn: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) When request was initially authored</li>
<li>reasonCode: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Reason or indication for ordering or not ordering the medication</li>
<li>reasonReference: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) US Quality Core Condition or Observation that supports the prescription</li>
<li>dosageInstruction: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) How medication should be taken</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.text: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Free text dosage instructions e.g. SIG</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) When medication should be administered</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat: (USCDI+ Quality) When the event is to occur</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.bounds[x]: (USCDI+ Quality) Length/Range of lengths, or (Start and/or end) limits</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.frequency: (USCDI+ Quality) Event occurs frequency times per period</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.frequencyMax: (USCDI+ Quality) Event occurs frequencyMax times per period</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.period: (USCDI+ Quality) Event occurs frequency times per period</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.periodMax: (USCDI+ Quality) Upper limit of period (3-4 hours)</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.periodUnit: (USCDI+ Quality) s | min | h | d | wk | mo | a - unit of time (UCUM)</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.asNeeded[x]: (USCDI+ Quality) Take "as needed" (for x)</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.route: (USCDI+ Quality) How drug should enter body</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.doseAndRate: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Amount of medication administered</li>
<li>dosageInstruction.doseAndRate.dose[x]: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Amount of medication per dose</li>
<li>dispenseRequest: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Medication supply authorization</li>
<li>dispenseRequest.validityPeriod: (USCDI+ Quality) Time period supply is authorized for</li>
<li>dispenseRequest.numberOfRepeatsAllowed: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Number of refills authorized</li>
<li>dispenseRequest.quantity: (USCDI+ Quality) (USCDI) Amount of medication to supply per dispense</li>
<li>dispenseRequest.expectedSupplyDuration: (USCDI+ Quality) Number of days supply per dispense</li>
</ul>
<!-- END AUTOGENERATED KEY ELEMENTS -->

Because this profile is derived from US Core, systems are also expected to support Must Support requirements and (USCDI) tagged elements inherited from US Core. The [Formal Views](#profile) section also provides the formal summary, definitions, terminology requirements, and mandatory element requirements.

<h3>Profile Specific Implementation Guidance</h3>

<p>To represent that a medication request was intentionally not requested for a medical, patient, or system reason, use the <a href="StructureDefinition-us-quality-core-medicationnotrequested.html">US Quality Core Medication Not Requested</a> profile.</p>

<p>Additional informational guidance for authoring CQL using this profile is provided in the <a href="#cql-authoring-usage-informational">CQL Authoring Usage (Informational)</a> section of this page.</p>
